commodities.name: Wheat
commodities.detail: Wheat futures $6.65/bushel (Chicago SRW, May 21-22 — up 28.75 cents on session). Kansas City HRW: $7.04/bushel. USDA season-average farm price forecast $6.50/bu (3-year high, +$1.50 YoY). May WASDE bullish estimates — lowest all-wheat production since 1972/73. Fertilizer crisis and fuel cost passthrough continue adding risk premium. Pre-war: $5.62/bushel.
commodities.source: AgWeb / Farm Futures — Afternoon market recap May 21

commodities.name: Corn
commodities.detail: Corn futures $4.66/bushel (CBOT, May 20-21 — TradingEconomics: fell to 465.78 cents/bu on May 20, -1.99% on day). Disrupted fertilizer supply (nitrogen/urea up 21%) continues to hurt corn economics. USDA projects corn acreage down ~3% in 2026 vs 2025. Pre-war: $4.31/bushel.

commodities.name: Soybeans
commodities.detail: Soybeans $11.93/bushel (May 21-22, TradingEconomics — down 0.54% on day, steadied after surge May 18). USDA WASDE May: 2026-27 soybean stocks 310M bushels — below analyst estimates. EPA biofuel mandate boosting demand. Soybean oil at historic highs.

commodities.name: Rice
commodities.detail: Asian import routes through Persian Gulf completely disrupted. Export restrictions emerging in Southeast Asia.

commodities.name: Sugar
commodities.detail: ICE Sugar #11 at $0.224/lb (April 25). Up 6.7% from pre-war. USDA forecasts sugar & sweets +6.7% in 2026.

commodities.name: Cooking Oil
commodities.detail: Vegetable oil price index at highest since June 2022. Palm oil (CPO June) RM 4,535/tonne (April 11, down 2.33% — five-week rally snapped). Indonesia CPO IDR 16,049/kg.

commodities.name: Coffee
commodities.detail: Arabica $2.83/lb (May 1). Down from 2025 peak of $4.41 — Brazil 2026/27 harvest projected +12% easing pressure. Down 27.5% YoY. Container rates still up 85% on key routes.

commodities.name: Beef
commodities.detail: Feed costs up due to grain price spikes. Diesel-dependent supply chain passing costs to consumers.

groceryImpact.usdaForecast2026: 2.5% food-at-home (USDA May update), but rising energy costs may push higher
groceryImpact.note: Day 80: BLS April 2026: food-at-home prices +0.7% MoM (largest single-month jump in ~4 years), +2.4% YoY. USDA revised 2026 food inflation forecast: +3.6% overall; beef +10.1%. Fresh vegetables: +44% annualized over past 3 months. UN Food Price Index April: +1.6% MoM (3-year high). Wheat $6.65/bushel. Urea fertilizer $577/tonne (+21% vs pre-war $475). Iran PGSA operational — 35% of global urea supply from Middle East remains blocked. Container shipping rates: Shanghai→Rotterdam +11% WoW at $2,413/40ft. If Hormuz persists through summer, could add 5-7 pp to food inflation. 318M face hunger globally (FAO).

fertilizer.detail: Urea pulled back to $577/tonne by May 15 (down from April peak of $698/tonne). Still up +21% from pre-war $475/tonne. World Bank projects 60% full-year 2026 increase in fertilizer prices. Half a million tonnes of nitrogen fertilizer not being produced. Middle East supplies 35% of global urea exports — all blocked by Hormuz closure. Ammonia prices up 41% YoY. 25% of US farmers have NOT purchased fertilizer for planting season.

context: Hormuz Strait closure (Day 80) continues to severely impact global food supply chains. Fertilizer costs have effectively doubled for US agriculture. Grocery store prices up 0.7% in April — biggest 1-month jump in 4 years. USDA forecasts food-at-home prices +3.6% for full year 2026. Iraq militia drone attacks on UAE Barakah nuclear plant add new regional instability risk.
